task_completion.description: Open the edit transaction dialog for the MSFT stock.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Buy Transaction' (or similar edit) dialog is open specifically for the MSFT stock (50 units).
The value '9.99' is entered into the Broker Fee field in the edit dialog.
The value '0.03' is entered into the Clearing Fee field in the edit dialog.

task_completion.description: Update the Comment to 'Q1 2024 core holding'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Q1 2024 core holding' is entered into the Comment field in the edit dialog.

task_completion.description: Save the transaction and verify it appears correctly in the portfolio alongside other unchanged transactions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The edit dialog is closed, and the main JStock Portfolio Management tab shows the MSFT row updated with the new fees and comment. The AAPL and NVDA rows must still be visible and unchanged.

integrity.description: The agent used the JStock graphical user interface to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the JStock application windows and dialogs, not a text editor.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the GUI by editing the underlying CSV file directly.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows are shown using commands like nano, vim, sed, or echo to modify the buyportfolio.csv file.

integrity.description: Other portfolio fields and transactions were left completely unchanged.
integrity.visual_evidence: The AAPL and NVDA transactions remain exactly as they were, and no other fields in the MSFT transaction (like date, price, or units) were altered.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target transaction: MSFT. Expected Broker Fee: 9.99. Expected Clearing Fee: 0.03. Expected Comment: 'Q1 2024 core holding'. The edits must be made via the JStock GUI, and the AAPL and NVDA transactions must remain unchanged.
